acceptance_criteria_summary: Runnable R and Python pseudocode present and consistent with checklist
Reproducibility checklist enumerates filenames and software versions
Quantified sensitivity analysis for S2 included
JSON validation evidence included in trace

extra_model_fields.risk_notes: Adding runnable snippets requires careful file/path naming to avoid divergence from the checklist; ensure snippet filenames match the checklist exactly.
verifier_recommended_fixes: Reconcile and deduplicate repeated grader fields so a single authoritative 'passed' value exists

acceptance_criteria: Each snippet is <= 20 lines and uses the exact filenames listed in reproducibility checklist
Snippets include package imports and a single command to run the batch method
planned_changes.description: Add two short runnable pseudocode blocks (R and Python) demonstrating: loading samples.tsv and intensities.tsv, applying the QC rule, filtering out failed samples, log-transforming intensities, applying within-sample median centering, and calling a batch-correction method (ComBat or lm residual).

acceptance_criteria: Checklist lists: samples.tsv with sample_id, reads (int), mito_pct (float), detected_genes (int), batch (optional); intensities.tsv with metabolite_id row and sample_id columns; recommended R/Python versions and package versions
Checklist references the exact paths used by the pseudocode
planned_changes.description: Insert an explicit reproducibility checklist enumerating expected input filenames, required columns (names and types), recommended encoding, and software/runtime versions with minimum package versions.

acceptance_criteria: At least three perturbation scenarios shown with numeric tables
Clear conclusion for each scenario (PASS/FAIL and rationale)
planned_changes.description: Provide quantified perturbation scenarios for S2 (reads, mito_pct, detected_genes), show resulting boolean QC outcomes and state the impact on inclusion/exclusion and recommended next steps (e.g., retest, include in sensitivity model).

acceptance_criteria: Includes at least PCA and per-batch summary examples with corresponding plotting commands
Explains what to look for in each diagnostic and pass/fail heuristics
planned_changes.description: Add commands/pseudocode for basic diagnostics (PCA before/after, batch-wise means/variances, per-feature variance explained) and guidance for interpreting these diagnostics.

acceptance_criteria: trace contains the exact validation command used and its stdout
actual_outputs.json summary lists the new artifact sections and filenames
deliverable: updated attempts/baseline/agent_trace.json (or new trace for revised attempt)
updated actual_outputs.json for the revised attempt
planned_changes.description: Re-run JSON validation, include the validator command and stdout (e.g., 'JSON_OK') in the trace, and update actual_outputs.json summary to reference the new sections and filenames.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Improve reproducibility and validation credit by adding runnable pseudocode, an explicit reproducibility checklist, and quantified sensitivity analysis for borderline sample S2; also include post-correction diagnostics and JSON validation evidence.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Solid baseline: clear per-sample QC extraction, exact arithmetic, and a defensible decision to exclude S2. Shortcomings that limit reproducibility and validation credit: (1) no runnable commands or pseudocode showing how to load inputs, apply the QC gate, normalize, and run batch-correction (ri_2); (2) sensitivity/validation work is qualitative-no quantified perturbations or worked scenarios showing how 'needs_review' values would alter decisions (ri_4). Additionally, grader/verifier outputs contain inconsistent pass flags (internal metadata issue) that should be reconciled outside the artifact to avoid confusion.
